claim_status: No-claim planning inventory only; no production-safe browsing claim, no supported security versions claim, no incident-response readiness claim, no emergency patch capacity claim, no disclosure authority claim, no stable promotion authority claim, no signing authority claim, no incident closure authority claim, and no implementation claim.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Private report access must be restricted to named incident roles with audit records.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Access control evidence does not grant disclosure authority, stable promotion, signing authority, or incident closure.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Block public handling and require owner review when report access cannot be restricted.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Acknowledgement timing, minimum response content, and private-channel preservation must be rehearsed.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Acknowledgement cannot promise severity, disclosure timing, stable support, patch timing, supported security versions, or production-safe browsing.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Hold response content to a minimal receipt when authority or facts are incomplete.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: A private reproduction record must capture affected commit, platform, configuration, and sanitized artifact references.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Reproduction evidence does not decide severity, disclosure timing, stable promotion, or signing.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Quarantine exploit details and keep the issue private when reproduction is incomplete or unsafe.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Severity triage must record asset impact, boundary crossed, reachability, prerequisites, and confidence.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Agents and implementation authors cannot decide final vulnerability severity or disclosure timing.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Escalate to security owner review when severity evidence is missing, contradictory, or agent-derived.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Asset analysis must identify origin, profile, credential, update, signing, file, network, extension, DevTools, and agent assets at risk.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Asset analysis does not approve public disclosure, incident closure, or stable release readiness.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Preserve private handling and block closure when reachable assets are uncertain.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Affected-version statement must tie commits, builds, packages, channels, and unsupported unknowns to one private record.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Affected-version drafting does not create supported security versions, disclosure authority, stable support, or patch SLAs.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: State unknowns privately and block public support language when version scope is unproven.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Embargo scope, participants, evidence locations, escalation, and expiry triggers must be rehearsed.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Embargo handling does not authorize disclosure timing, public issue conversion, or release promotion.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Keep details private and escalate when embargo scope or participant authority is unclear.

private_intake_steps.required_evidence: Sanitized evidence preservation must remove secrets, credentials, private-session data, and exploit-enabling payloads before sharing.
private_intake_steps.authority_boundary: Sanitized evidence cannot hide severity, affected versions, disclosure risk, incident closure blockers, or residual risk from reviewers.
private_intake_steps.safe_failure: Redact or discard unsafe evidence payloads and keep original exploit material access-restricted.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Protected patch branch creation, review rules, CI visibility, and restricted access must be rehearsed.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: A protected branch does not grant self-approval, signing authority, stable promotion, or disclosure authority.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block patch work or keep it private when branch protections cannot be verified.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Embargoed CI must run required checks without exposing exploit details or secrets to public logs.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Embargoed CI success does not prove production-safe browsing, supported security versions, stable support, or release readiness.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Stop the rehearsal and restrict logs when CI leaks details or cannot run privately.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Regression test evidence must prove the reported path fails safely without publishing exploit-enabling details.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: A regression test does not replace independent review, fuzzing, sandbox evidence, disclosure review, or stable release gates.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block closure and preserve private handling when regression evidence is absent or too narrow.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Backport decision evidence must record supported branches, unsupported branches, risk, and owner decision.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Backport notes do not create supported security versions, stable support, disclosure authority, or patch-time guarantees.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Mark support scope blocked when branch support, owner coverage, or patch applicability is unknown.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Signing and update dry run must use non-production keys and tie artifacts to update metadata and validation output.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Dry-run evidence cannot use production signing keys, offline roots, stable channels, or public distribution.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Reject the dry run and preserve no-production-key boundaries when signing or update authority is ambiguous.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Staged rollout rehearsal must record cohort, pause, rollback, failure, and communication decision points.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Staged rollout rehearsal does not approve public distribution, stable promotion, or emergency patch capacity.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Abort rollout rehearsal when pause, rollback, or owner authority is missing.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Minimum secure version rehearsal must block known vulnerable versions in update and rollback paths.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: A version-floor plan does not create supported security versions, stable support, or signing authority without staffing and update capacity.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block rollback or install when a version is revoked, vulnerable, or below the floor.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Revocation rehearsal must record revoked artifacts, metadata, keys or channels, and client behavior.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Revocation planning does not authorize production signing key rotation, stable update service changes, or public update service changes.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block install, update, or rollback when revocation status cannot be verified.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Release notes must balance affected-version facts, mitigation guidance, credit, and exploit-detail restraint.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Draft notes do not approve disclosure timing, public claims, or supported security versions.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Hold public notes when disclosure owner, severity, affected version, or mitigation wording is unresolved.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: User and admin communication rehearsal must cover update urgency, mitigation, unsupported cases, and support channels.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Communication rehearsal does not create support capacity, disclosure authority, stable support, patch-time guarantees, or release readiness.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block publication when support, legal, disclosure, or release owner approval is missing.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: CVE and credit handling must record reporter preference, CNA path, affected versions, and coordinated timing.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: CVE handling does not let agents decide severity, disclosure, or incident closure.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Escalate to security and legal owners when CVE scope, credit, or timing is unclear.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Coordinated disclosure rehearsal must record parties, embargo status, public timing, and protected-user state.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Disclosure timing remains owner-controlled and unavailable to implementation agents.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Keep the issue private when users are not protected or disclosure authority is missing.

emergency_patch_steps.required_evidence: Postmortem remediation must record root cause, systemic fixes, tests, docs, owners, and follow-up deadlines.
emergency_patch_steps.authority_boundary: Postmortem drafting does not grant incident closure authority or waive unresolved stable release-critical findings.
emergency_patch_steps.safe_failure: Block closure when root cause, systemic remediation, owner, or regression evidence is missing.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse private escalation, user protection, patch path, communication timing, and evidence preservation for active exploitation.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: No current staffing, update capacity, or supported-version evidence proves emergency response capacity.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Keep production-safe and supported-security claims blocked until response capacity is proven.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse update metadata compromise, signing compromise, revocation, minimum secure version, and recovery ownership.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: No production key ceremony, offline root, stable channel, or real updater exists.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Block release and update-trust claims until production key and update recovery evidence exists.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse advisory intake, affected component analysis, source rebuild, SBOM impact, and emergency dependency replacement.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Dependency response capacity and supported-version policy remain unproven.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Block supported-security claims when dependency advisory ownership or rebuild evidence is missing.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse preservation, affected-version analysis, user communication, recovery guidance, and migration rollback boundaries.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Data-loss safety and profile migration safety remain unproven.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Block closure and release claims when data-loss scope or recovery evidence is incomplete.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse secret redaction, affected asset analysis, notification decision, provider exposure, and log cleanup.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Privacy leak response, notification authority, and evidence redaction process remain unproven.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Keep details private and block disclosure until privacy, legal, and security owners review.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse compromised-process proof, sandbox probe failure, mitigation, affected platform, and release-blocker handling.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Platform sandbox proof and expected-deny probe packages remain incomplete.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Treat the regression as a release blocker until platform and security owners accept evidence.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse extension/provider disablement, grant revocation, user communication, audit preservation, and policy update.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Extension/provider systems and authority revocation evidence remain future work.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Block extension, provider, and agent safety claims when revocation behavior is unproven.

incident_classes.tabletop_scope: Rehearse update, sync, account, support, or optional-service outage handling with local browsing fallback.
incident_classes.blocking_gap: Service dependency, offline behavior, support capacity, and stable SLO evidence remain unproven.
incident_classes.safe_failure: Block service-readiness and stable-support claims until outage behavior is tested.

role_matrix.responsibility: Own incident state, acceptance criteria, closure recommendation, and unresolved-risk escalation.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Owner cannot bypass independent review, legal approval, signing controls, or release gates.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Named qualified owner and backup coverage remain required before readiness claims.

role_matrix.responsibility: Independently review reproduction, patch, regression, affected-version scope, and residual risk.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Reviewer cannot self-approve their own implementation or decide disclosure timing alone.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Independent reviewer assignment and review records remain required.

role_matrix.responsibility: Coordinate package identity, update dry run, staged rollout, minimum secure version, revocation, and release notes.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Release role has no production signing authority or stable promotion authority without owner controls.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Release capacity, signed update evidence, and supported-version policy remain required.

role_matrix.responsibility: Classify severity, assets, exploitability, embargo scope, disclosure risk, and regression sufficiency.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Security role cannot waive stable-critical boundaries or hide unsupported security status.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Security reviewer coverage, severity process, and tabletop output remain required.

role_matrix.responsibility: Review coordinated disclosure, CVE/credit, regulatory, notification, and public wording risks.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Legal review does not create security readiness, release readiness, or technical closure.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Legal-community backup ownership and disclosure approval path remain required.

role_matrix.responsibility: Prepare user and admin communication, mitigation guidance, support intake, and known-risk messaging.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: Support cannot promise supported security versions or patch targets before gates pass.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: Support staffing, response targets, and communication rehearsal remain required.

role_matrix.responsibility: Track timing targets, escalation path, handoff, availability, and secret-rotation coordination.
role_matrix.authority_boundary: On-call does not grant signing authority, stable promotion authority, disclosure authority, or incident closure authority.
role_matrix.missing_evidence: On-call staffing, escalation drills, secret rotation, and backup coverage remain required.
